name: Limburg (Belgium)

colors.symbolism: the field of the historic arms of the Duchy of Limburg
the lion's tongue and claws, the ducal coronet, and the gold stripes of the heart shield (Loon)

description: A white field charged centrally with a red lion rampant with yellow tongue and claws, wearing a yellow ducal coronet, and bearing on its body a small escutcheon of ten horizontal stripes alternating yellow and red.
history: Adopted as the provincial flag in 1996 alongside the new official coat of arms. The red lion on a white field derives from the medieval Duchy of Limburg; the ducal coronet recalls the duchy's rank, and the burely (ten-bar) heart shield references the County of Loon, whose territory makes up most of the modern Belgian province.

features.role: Limburg lion, rampant lion with yellow tongue and claws, wearing a yellow ducal coronet, charged on the body with a burely escutcheon of ten yellow and red stripes (the arms of Loon)
features.symbolism: the lion of the medieval Duchy of Limburg, defaced with a small ten-bar heart shield for the historic County of Loon
